content.text:
```mdl
@ NON‑NEGOTIABLE CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON‑DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT – TRANSPORTATION CHALLENGE 2017
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_9_nda_12

open std.collections

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Types that capture the domain objects described in the Agreement
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Definition of a person (used for contacts and representatives)
type Person = {
    name: string,
    email: string,
    phone: string
}

@ BPS contact person (the “notified” representative)
type BPSContact = Person

@ Vendor representative (employee, subcontractor, etc.)
type Representative = Person

@ Vendor entity – the “Receiving Party”
type Vendor = {
    name: string,
    representatives: List<Representative>,
    -- purpose for which confidential data may be used
    purpose: string,                         -- e.g. "BPS_service"
    -- flags that are set by the vendor to satisfy obligations
    disclosed_to_third_party: bool,
    notified_bps_of_legal_process: bool,
    returned_or_destroyed_confidential_info: bool,
    background_check_conducted: bool,
    may_develop_similar_info: bool           -- permission to develop independent data
}

@ BPS entity – the “Disclosing Party”
type BPS = {
    name: string,
    address: string,
    contact: BPSContact
}

@ Confidential information that is subject to the NDA
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    student_home_address: string,
    latitude: rat,
    longitude: rat,
    school_assignment: string,
    ridership_status: string,   -- corner stop, D2D, wheelchair, …
    grade: int,
    bus_stop_intersection: string,
    bus_stop_lat: rat,
    bus_stop_long: rat,
    survey_data: string
}

@ Database used by the vendor to store and process the confidential data
type Database = {
    encryption_at_rest: bool,
    encryption_in_transit: bool,
    access_control: bool,
    audit_log_enabled: bool,
    firewall_enabled: bool,
    antivirus_installed: bool
}

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Entities – the stable public anchors for alignment with other standards
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Party definitions (the two “Parties” to the Agreement)
entity vendor: Vendor
entity bps: BPS

@ The confidential data set that the vendor receives
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

@ The secure database that the vendor must maintain
entity database: Database

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Helper functions (pure, no side‑effects)
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Check that the vendor’s declared purpose matches the allowed purpose
func purpose_is_allowed(purpose: string) -> bool:
    purpose = "BPS_service"

@ Check that the database satisfies the required security controls
func db_meets_security(db: Database) -> bool:
    db.encryption_at_rest and
    db.encryption_in_transit and
    db.access_control and
    db.audit_log_enabled and
    db.firewall_enabled and
    db.antivirus_installed

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Obligations (O), Prohibitions (F) and Permissions (P)
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause B.1 – Data Sharing – security obligations
rule O vendor_maintains_secure_database:
    db_meets_security(database) always

@ Clause B.2 – Nondisclosure – purpose limitation
rule O vendor_uses_data_only_for_allowed_purpose:
    purpose_is_allowed(vendor.purpose) always

@ Clause B.2 – Nondisclosure – prohibition on unauthorized disclosure
rule F vendor_may_not_disclose_without_consent:
    not vendor.disclosed_to_third_party always

@ Clause B.3 – Background Checks
rule O vendor_conducts_background_checks:
    vendor.background_check_conducted always

@ Clause B.4 – Disclosure Sought Under Legal Process – notification obligation
rule O vendor_notifies_bps_of_legal_process:
    vendor.notified_bps_of_legal_process always

@ Clause B.6 – Return or Destruction of Materials
rule O vendor_returns_or_destroys_confidential_info:
    vendor.returned_or_destroyed_confidential_info always

@ Clause B.9 – Injunctive Relief – implied prohibition on breach (modelled as a prohibition)
rule F vendor_may_not_breach_obligations:
    not (
        vendor.disclosed_to_third_party or
        not vendor.notified_bps_of_legal_process or
        not vendor.returned_or_destroyed_confidential_info
    ) always

@ Clause B.10 – Immediate Notice – same as notification rule (already covered)

@ Clause B.12 – Termination – obligations survive termination (modelled as always)
rule O vendor_obligations_survive_termination:
    purpose_is_allowed(vendor.purpose) and
    db_meets_security(database) and
    not vendor.disclosed_to_third_party and
    vendor.notified_bps_of_legal_process and
    vendor.returned_or_destroyed_confidential_info and
    vendor.background_check_conducted always

@ Hypothesis – Receiving Party may independently develop information similar to Confidential Information
rule P vendor_may_develop_similar_information:
    vendor.may_develop_similar_info always

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- End of module
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
